title: Hydrogen Industry Groups Push for Offshore Electrolysis to Be Part of Germanyâ€™s Expansion Strategy
content: In a unified appeal to the German government, a coalition of industry associations â€” including BDEW, AquaVentus, BEE, BWO, DWV, DVGW, FNB Gas, EEHH, EESH, EnergiekÃ¼ste SH, HYPOS, WAB, the VDMA Association, and IG Metall (Coast District) â€” is calling for offshore hydrogen production through electrolysis to be fully incorporated into the countryâ€™s offshore wind expansion framework. This joint position, branded as the â€œHydrogen Eightâ€ initiative, aims to address the gap in current planning that overlooks the role offshore electrolysis could play in advancing both hydrogen supply and wind energy cost-efficiency. The industry coalition highlighted that the ongoing update of Germanyâ€™s offshore spatial development plan (FEP), particularly for Zones 4 and 5 of the German exclusive economic zone (EEZ), fails to include offshore electrolysis in its strategy. This, they argue, is a missed opportunity.
author: Analysts Account
